docs.title: Predictive Pod Autoscaler (PPA)
docs.text: Predictive Pod Autoscaler (PPA) is an AI-driven horizontal pod autoscaling solution for Kubernetes. It leverages Facebook's Prophet for time-series forecasting to proactively scale your workloads based on historical CPU and memory usage patterns.

docs.title: Why PPA?
docs.text: Standard Kubernetes HPA and KEDA scalers are reactive—they scale up when a threshold is breached. PPA is proactive—it anticipates traffic spikes and scales your application before the load hits, ensuring better performance and availability.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• 🧠 Predictive Scaling: Uses Prophet for accurate time-series forecasting.
  • ⚡ KEDA Integration: Works as an External Scaler for KEDA, allowing you to use it alongside other KEDA scalers.
  • 🔄 Automated Training: Hourly retraining of models using Argo Workflows ensures your scaling logic adapts to changing patterns.
  • 🚀 Cloud-Native Architecture: Built on top of KServe, MLServer, and Argo Workflows.
  • 🛠️ Declarative Management: Define your predictive scaling models using a simple Custom Resource (Model).

docs.title: How it Works
docs.text:
1. Fetch: PPA fetches historical metrics (CPU/Memory) from Prometheus.
  2. Train: An Argo Workflow trains a Prophet model on the collected data.
  3. Serve: The trained model is deployed as a KServe InferenceService.
  4. Scale: The PPA Scaler queries the InferenceService and tells KEDA how many replicas are needed.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Predictive Pod Autoscaler (PPA) is designed as a modular, cloud-native system that integrates several industry-standard tools.

docs.title: High-Level Architecture
docs.text:
The following diagram illustrates how the components of PPA interact:

graph TD
    subgraph "Control Plane"
        CRD[Model CRD]
        KRO[Kro Controller]
        Argo[Argo Workflows]
    end

    subgraph "Data Plane"
        Prom[Prometheus]
        S3[S3 Storage / SeaweedFS]
        KS[KServe / MLServer]
    end

    subgraph "Scaling Engine"
        KEDA[KEDA]
        Scaler[PPA Scaler gRPC]
    end

    CRD --> KRO
    KRO --> Argo
    Argo --> Prom
    Argo --> S3
    Argo --> KS
    Scaler --> CRD
    Scaler --> KS
    KEDA --> Scaler
    KEDA --> Workload[Target Workload]

docs.title: 1. Model CRD (managed by Kro)
docs.text: PPA uses Kro to define the Model Custom Resource. When you create a Model, Kro automatically orchestrates: - An Initial Training Workflow (Argo). - A Cron Workflow for hourly retraining (Argo). - A KServe InferenceService to serve the model.

docs.title: 2. PPA Scaler
docs.text: A gRPC-based External Scaler for KEDA. It acts as the bridge between KEDA and the predictive models. - IsActive: Determines if the workload should be active (avoiding scale-to-zero if a load is predicted). - GetMetrics: Queries the KServe InferenceService for a prediction and returns the recommended replica count.

docs.title: 3. PPA Pipeline
docs.text: A set of Python scripts packaged as a Docker image that: - Connects to Prometheus to fetch historical metrics. - Uses Prophet to train a time-series forecasting model. - Packages and uploads the model to S3-compatible storage.

docs.title: 4. PPA Runtime
docs.text: A custom MLServer runtime designed specifically to load Prophet models and perform inference based on a "horizon" parameter (how far into the future to predict).

docs.title: 5. Argo Workflows
docs.text: Orchestrates the training pipeline. It handles data fetching, model training, and updating the InferenceService.

docs.title: 6. KServe
docs.text: Provides the infrastructure for serving the predictive models at scale, supporting features like scale-to-zero for the predictors themselves.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Predictive Pod Autoscaler (PPA) is a proactive horizontal pod autoscaling solution. This section will guide you through the prerequisites and setup process.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before installing PPA, ensure you have the following components running in your cluster:

  1. Kubernetes Cluster: v1.25 or newer.
  2. Helm: v3.x.
  3. KEDA: For horizontal pod autoscaling.
  4. Argo Workflows: For orchestrating the training pipelines.
  5. KServe: For serving the predictive models.
  6. Kro: For managing the Model CRD and its associated resources.
  7. Prometheus: To provide historical metrics for training.
  8. S3-Compatible Storage: (e.g., SeaweedFS, Minio, or AWS S3) for storing model artifacts.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Follow the guides below to get PPA up and running:

  • Installation: Learn how to deploy PPA using our OCI Helm chart.
  • Usage Guide: Learn how to define models and scale your workloads.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: PPA is installed via Helm using our OCI registry.

docs.title: 1. Configuration
docs.text:
Create a values.yaml file to configure PPA for your environment. At a minimum, you need to provide your S3 storage credentials.

s3:
  endpoint: s3.your-domain.com
  bucket: ppa-models
  accessKey: YOUR_ACCESS_KEY
  secretKey: YOUR_SECRET_KEY
  insecure: false

docs.title: 2. Installing the Chart
docs.text:
To install version 0.1.9 of PPA, use the oci:// endpoint:

kubectl create namespace ppa
helm install ppa oci://ghcr.io/cloudstack0/predictive-pod-autoscaler/ppa --version 0.1.9 -n ppa -f values.yaml

Quick Install (Optional Dependencies)

The PPA Helm chart can optionally install KEDA, Argo Workflows, and SeaweedFS for you: 

helm install ppa oci://ghcr.io/cloudstack0/predictive-pod-autoscaler/ppa --version 0.1.9 \
  --set keda.enabled=true \
  --set argo-workflows.enabled=true \
  --set seaweedfs.enabled=true

docs.title: 3. Verification
docs.text:
Once installed, check that the PPA Scaler is running:

kubectl get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=ppa-scaler -n ppa

You should also see the ResourceGraphDefinition for the Model CRD:

kubectl get resourcegraphdefinitions

docs.title: Upgrading
docs.text:
To upgrade PPA to a newer version:

helm upgrade ppa oci://ghcr.io/cloudstack0/predictive-pod-autoscaler/ppa --version 0.1.9 -n ppa -f values.yaml

docs.title: Uninstallation
docs.text:
To uninstall PPA:

helm uninstall ppa -n ppa

docs.title: Helm Reference
docs.text: This page provides a detailed reference of all configurable parameters for the Predictive Pod Autoscaler (PPA) Helm chart.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Repository Name Version https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm argo-workflows 1.0.13 https://kedacore.github.io/charts keda 2.19.0 https://seaweedfs.github.io/seaweedfs/helm seaweedfs 3.52.0

docs.text:
Key Type Default Description argo-workflows.enabled bool false keda.enabled bool false pipeline.image.pullPolicy string "IfNotPresent" pipeline.image.repository string "ghcr.io/cloudstack0/predictive-pod-autoscaler/pipeline" pipeline.image.tag string "latest" runtime.image.pullPolicy string "IfNotPresent" runtime.image.repository string "ghcr.io/cloudstack0/predictive-pod-autoscaler/runtime" runtime.image.tag string "latest" runtime.resources.limits.cpu string "1" runtime.resources.limits.memory string "2Gi" runtime.resources.requests.cpu string "1" runtime.resources.requests.memory string "2Gi" s3.accessKey string "admin" s3.bucket string "ppa" s3.endpoint string "seaweedfs-s3.seaweedfs:8333" s3.insecure bool true s3.secretKey string "admin123" scaler.enabled bool true scaler.image.pullPolicy string "IfNotPresent" scaler.image.repository string "ghcr.io/cloudstack0/predictive-pod-autoscaler/scaler" scaler.image.tag string "latest" scaler.replicas int 1 scaler.resources.limits.cpu string "500m" scaler.resources.limits.memory string "512Mi" scaler.resources.requests.cpu string "250m" scaler.resources.requests.memory string "256Mi" scaler.securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation bool false scaler.securityContext.capabilities.drop[0] string "ALL" scaler.securityContext.runAsGroup int 1000 scaler.securityContext.runAsNonRoot bool true scaler.securityContext.runAsUser int 1000 scaler.securityContext.seccompProfile.type string "RuntimeDefault" scaler.service.port int 9090 scaler.service.type string "ClusterIP" seaweedfs.enabled bool false

docs.title: Usage Guide
docs.text: Using PPA involves defining a Model for your workload and then configuring a KEDA ScaledObject to use that model.

docs.title: 1. Define a Model
docs.text:
Create a Model resource to tell PPA which workload to track and where to get the metrics.

apiVersion: ppa.io/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: my-app-cpu-prediction
  namespace: my-apps
spec:
  prometheusUrl: "http://prometheus-operated.monitoring:9090"
  queryType: "cpu" # or "memory"
  workload:
    type: "deployment"
    name: "my-app"

What happens next? 1. Kro detects the new Model. 2. An Argo Workflow (my-app-cpu-prediction-init) is triggered immediately to train the first model. 3. A CronWorkflow is created to retrain the model every hour. 4. A KServe InferenceService named my-app-cpu-prediction is created to serve the model.

docs.title: 2. Configure KEDA ScaledObject
docs.text:
Now, create a KEDA ScaledObject that uses the PPA External Scaler.

apiVersion: keda.sh/v1alpha1
kind: ScaledObject
metadata:
  name: my-app-scaler
  namespace: my-apps
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    name: my-app
  minReplicaCount: 1
  maxReplicaCount: 10
  triggers:
    - type: external-push
      metadata:
        scalerAddress: ppa-scaler.ppa:9090
        modelName: my-app-cpu-prediction
        horizon: "5"      # Predict 5 minutes into the future
        targetValue: "0.8" # Target 80% CPU usage

docs.title: 3. Monitoring
docs.text:
You can monitor the training progress by looking at the Argo Workflows:

# Check workflow status
argo list -n my-apps

# Check model status and inference URL
kubectl get models my-app-cpu-prediction -o yaml
